Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Is there a defect in lmd_find_delimiter() that I'm not seeing? If so then could you explain it?

Patch Set 2:

This is a patch to make the code kernel compliant. Directly parsing strings like we are doing is forbidden. I like to push the earlier fixes upstream and I want to run the change by people here to make sure its okay. I had that in the earlier commit message but you didn't like that. What are you looking for?

Patch Set 2:

This is just way more complicated (and harder to maintain or modify) than before. And why? Because some German guy sitting in his mom's basement said not to do our own string parsing. That's not a good enough reason for me.

Patch Set 2:

Tests uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-36vm5 for review-ldiskfs received by maloo, run on CentOS Linux 7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/822e52b4-1686-4ab6-8653-ef53fa6d5002). Ran 4 tests. No failures.

Patch Set 2:

If I come up with a simpler patch will you be okay with it or will you always reject it due to the reason behind this work?

Patch Set 2:

> If I come up with a simpler patch will you be okay with it or will you always reject it due to the reason behind this work?

Do you think I'm rejecting it because of the reason? Please look at lmd_find_delimiter() before and after your patch and explain why this is better. I think in this case you should be pushing back against Greg and not me.

Patch Set 2:

Alright. When the widow opens up upstream I will push again and then push back.

parents.subject: LU-9230 ldlm: speed up preparation for list of lock cancel

commit.subject: LU-9325 obdclass: handle strings correctly in lmd_find_delimiter
commit.message:
LU-9325 obdclass: handle strings correctly in lmd_find_delimiter

The feedback from the pushing of the lmd_find_delimiter() work
upstream was not to directly parse the string data passed in.

Change the return value to a bool and return true when the
character is found. Currently even though this function is
named lmd_find_delimiter() it return 1 when nothing is found
which is counter intuitive. Use strcspn() to determine the
position where the first delimiter is found. Add a test to
make sure the mount string is valid.

Change-Id: I7ef53f29a6d3284acd01225c115712d3674e8435
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/26558
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
